final_message:
All five functions are implemented in `problems.py` and verified.

## Summary

| Function | Approach | Key edge cases handled |
|---|---|---|
| `parse_duration` | `re.fullmatch` over an ordered `(h)?(m)?(s)?` pattern | non-string → `ValueError`; out-of-order, duplicates, unknown units, whitespace, trailing garbage, sign-less units all fail fullmatch naturally; "exceeds range" (`90m`) and multi-digit values allowed |
| `luhn_valid` | Double every second digit from the right, `-=9` if `>9`, sum mod 10 | strips spaces/hyphens; empty or non-digit → `False`; never raises (guards non-str input); `"0"` valid, other single digits invalid |
| `flatten` | Recursive walk, building `prefix+sep+key` | empty dicts contribute nothing at any depth; lists/non-dicts kept as-is; new result dict, input untouched |
| `top_k_frequent` | `Counter` + sort by `(-count, word)` | `k<=0` → `[]`; `k` over count returns all; case-sensitive; alpha tie-break |
| `rolling_max` | Monotonic `deque` of indices, O(n) | `window<=0` → `ValueError`; `window>len` → `[]`; matches brute force on 2000 random trials |

Verification:
- All docstring examples reproduce exactly.
- Every called-out invalid input raises `ValueError` (or returns `False` for `luhn_valid`).
- `rolling_max` matched a brute-force `max()` on 2,000 randomized trials and ran 1M elements (window 1000) in ~0.38s — well inside a tight budget.
- Only standard-library imports (`re`, `collections`); no signatures/docstrings changed; no other modules created; no commits made.
